_: GRIT — Group Resolution In Time. The canonical, daemonless play-loop for running a role-played world (or any multi-agent coordination) on pscale blocks. This seed is a POINTER: the canonical loop is bundled as a sentinel and read at pscale://grit (bsp(agent_id='pscale', block='grit')) — identical at every bsp-mcp instance, so a beach does not host its own copy. MOST acts are SIMPLE: perceive the pool's new public facts and the live intentions, act, self-commit a terse public fact — no dice, no resolver. A CHECK is the exception: a CONTEST gathers opposed intentions into a window and one participant resolves both and CLAIMS it; a solo TRIAL resolves at once on fixed seeded dice. The pool is public reality in identifiers; each participant's witnessed is their private narrative; names are earned in-fiction. Single-resolution is enforced atomically by the beach (the resolves_window claim), not by a daemon. SUPERSEDES the earlier library GRIT (the vapour-liquid-solid synthesis-kernel daemon, this seed's prior content) and the daemon-era GRIT generally (timer rounds + server dispatch + compressors) — both retired. Three branches: the mount contract, the two-tier split, the lineage.
1: Mounting a world on GRIT. GRIT supplies the loop only; a world supplies the rest. (1) Author a RESOLUTION-RULES block — the reference is NOMAD (capability + situation + dice − difficulty → outcome bands); a different system (D&D-style d20 + AC + HP) is a sibling rules block. (2) Author the PLACE — spatial:<world> (the map) + rules:<world> (place physics). (3) Author a CAST — each character a handle with passport + shell + witnessed + knows (block-conventions:2.5). (4) Open a room pool and point its underscore at the GRIT loop. Players and NPC agents inhabit characters via pscale_play, which inlines the loop every turn. The Designer (CADO) face edits the rules and world; GRIT itself never changes.
2: The two-tier split. GRIT (this loop — architect-owned, canonical, stable) sits above the engine (bsp + the beach) and below the world. NOMAD (the resolution rules) is the SWAPPABLE tier — replace it to change the dice without touching the loop. The WORLD (spatial + rules + cast) is content. A group implementing a different system keeps GRIT, swaps the rules block, authors their world. This is what lets quality come from players (via the Designer face) rather than the substrate architect.
3: Lineage. 'GRIT' once named a daemon — a synthesis kernel (vapour→liquid→solid, this seed's prior content) or a round-resolver (timer window + server dispatch + first-valid-wins + compressors). Both are retired: resolution is now in-loop in each participant's own LLM, gated by the beach's atomic single-resolution claim. The acronym still fits — groups resolve contested acts in time-windows — but the mechanism is daemonless. See pscale://grit (canonical) and bsp-mcp's proposals/2026-06-23-grit-extraction.md.
